sub_questions.question: Which password manager has the strongest security architecture and breach record for family use?
sub_questions.verdict_forced: Name a single product and defend the choice with its audit and breach history.

sub_questions.question: How do family-plan prices and seat limits compare across the leading candidates?
sub_questions.verdict_forced: State which product offers the best family-plan value at five seats.

sub_questions.question: Which product has the widest cross-platform and browser coverage for a mixed-device household?
sub_questions.verdict_forced: Name the product with the fewest platform gaps for a mixed-device family.

sub_questions.question: What is community sentiment on support quality and account-recovery experiences?
sub_questions.verdict_forced: State which product shows the healthiest support and recovery sentiment.

phase_2_prompts.ready_to_paste_prompt:
You are the Perplexity lane (evidence role) researching which password manager a small family should adopt. Sub-question SQ1: Which password manager has the strongest security architecture and breach record for family use? Begin your response with the exact line ===BEGIN LANE OUTPUT=== on its own line, before anything else — everything before it is treated as echoed prompt and ignored. Deliver six sections: TL;DR, Findings, Conflicts and uncertainties, What would change your recommendation, Sources consulted, Coverage gaps. Tag every factual claim [HIGH], [MEDIUM], or [LOW] by confidence; answering "insufficient evidence" is permitted and expected over guessing. Cite every factual claim with a LIVE, resolvable URL (https://...) and prioritise primary sources over aggregators; where sources conflict, present both positions. Do not use marketing copy or vendor self-promotion as evidence.
OUTPUT FORMAT (machine-checked — follow literally):
===BEGIN LANE OUTPUT===   <- exactly this, alone, as your very first line
## TL;DR
- <verdict bullet — three of these>
## Findings
1. [HIGH] <one-sentence finding>. Source: https://example.com/evidence
2. [MEDIUM] <next finding>. Source: https://example.org/report
3. [LOW] <third finding — at least 3 findings items>. Source: https://example.net/data
## Conflicts and uncertainties
## What would change your recommendation
## Sources consulted
- <at least 3 distinct https URLs, one per line>
## Coverage gaps
Headings are plain full lines with nothing after the name. Findings are plain "1." lines —
at least three of them, no blockquotes, no code fences anywhere in the answer — each with
its own [HIGH]/[MEDIUM]/[LOW] tag and a live https URL.

disqualifying_sources: Affiliate listicles with no hands-on testing methodology
Vendor comparison pages that rank their own product first
success_criteria: One recommended password manager supported by at least two independent evidence lanes
Every [HIGH] claim traceable to a primary source with a resolvable URL
known_traps: Conflating a browser extension audit with an audit of the core vault
Treating a bug-bounty payout as equivalent to a disclosed breach
